reasoning: An invasion intended to establish control over territory would require a substantially larger ground force than the ~10,000 troops currently deployed in the region. Experts (such as those from Chatham House and International Crisis Group) view the ongoing U.S. buildup, including the deployment of the USS Gerald R. Ford and lethal strikes on vessels, as a show of force, counter-narcotics operation, and signaling related to regime change or cartel activities, rather than preparation for a territorial invasion. With only two months left in 2025 and President Trump's historical aversion to nation-building or committing large ground forces, the timeline and logistics for a full land invasion are highly prohibitive. Therefore, the probability of an invasion intended to establish control over any portion of Venezuela by December 31, 2025, is very low.
